pattern.name: Sockhead Slouch Hat
pattern.sizes_available: baby, child, teen, adult small, medium, large, extra-large
pattern.gauge_description: 32 stitches and 46 rows = 4 inches in stockinette stitch
pattern.notes_html:
Version Four (V4) is now available! #sockheadhat
Also, try the condensed Quick Print version - it’s only one page!

This slouchy, comfy hat is the perfect project for that one skein of sock yarn that is way too special to put on your feet. Fair warning: there is miles of stockinette stitch, so this makes for great TV, travel or zen knitting.

The hat is simply constructed from the bottom to the crown. It is made up of a wide ribbed brim that folds up for extra warmth while the rest flops and folds over. The body of the hat is knit on short circular needles and the crown is closed up with double-pointed needles.

This seamless, unisex hat now has seven sizes (baby - adult extra-large). The pattern is very versatile, so go ahead and get creative!

Skill Level
Rookie-Apprentice-Virtuoso-Genius

Skills knit, purl, decreases

Sizes baby, child, teen, adult small, medium, large, extra-large

Finished Measurements
• adult medium is approx 23 cm/9” wide and 35 cm/14“ tall lying flat (unfolded); see chart in PDF for other sizes

Gauge
• approx 32 sts and 46 rows = 10 cm/4” in St stitch on 3 mm/US 2.5
• approx 28 sts = 10 cm/4“ in St st on 2.75 mm/US 2 as given on ball band

Yarn fingering weight alpaca blend

Suggested Brand
• 1 skein Misti Alpaca Hand Paint Sock Yarn
• 50% alpaca, 30% merino, 10% nylon, 10% silk
• 400 m (437 yds) per 100 g
• shown in colour 07 Miriam

Note: You may use any 100 g skein of fingering - sock - 4-ply yarn…see chart in PDF for amounts.

Needles
• 3 mm 40 cm (US 2.5 16”) circs
• 3 mm 40 cm (US 2.5) dpns
or size needed to obtain gauge

Notes: US 2.5 needles can be hard to find - you can use a US 3 instead. Some knitters prefer to use a smaller size needle for the ribbed brim only (like a 2.5 mm/US 2).

Other Materials
• tapestry needle or teeny crochet hook (for weaving in ends), stitch marker

Looking for a matching cowl? Try the Sockhead Cowl!

Or maybe some matching gloves: Pioneer Gloves!

Abbreviations

BOR = beginning of round
circ(s) = circular needle(s)
CO = cast on
dpn(s) = double pointed needle(s)
dec(s) = decrease(s)
K = knit
K2tog = knit two stitches together (decrease)
m = marker
P = purl
patt = pattern
pm = place marker
rem = remaining
rep = repeat
rnd(s) = round(s)
sl m = slip marker
st(s) = stitche(s)
St st = stockinette stitch / stocking stitch
tog = together
pattern.notes:
Version Four (V4) is now available! **#sockheadhat**
Also, try the condensed Quick Print version - it's only one page!

This slouchy, comfy hat is the perfect project for that one skein of sock yarn that is way too special to put on your feet. Fair warning: there is miles of stockinette stitch, so this makes for great TV, travel or zen knitting.

The hat is simply constructed from the bottom to the crown. It is made up of a wide ribbed brim that folds up for extra warmth while the rest flops and folds over. The body of the hat is knit on short circular needles and the crown is closed up with double-pointed needles.

This seamless, unisex hat now has seven sizes (baby - adult extra-large). The pattern is very versatile, so go ahead and get creative!   


**Skill Level
Rookie**-Apprentice-Virtuoso-Genius

**Skills** knit, purl, decreases

**Sizes** baby, child, teen, adult small, medium, large, extra-large

**Finished Measurements** 
• adult medium is approx 23 cm/9” wide and 35 cm/14“ tall lying flat (unfolded); see chart in PDF for other sizes

**Gauge** 
•  approx 32 sts and 46 rows = 10 cm/4” in St stitch on 3 mm/US 2.5
•  approx 28 sts = 10 cm/4“ in St st on 2.75 mm/US 2  as given on ball band

**Yarn** fingering weight alpaca blend 

**Suggested Brand**
•  1 skein Misti Alpaca Hand Paint Sock Yarn
•  50% alpaca, 30% merino, 10% nylon, 10% silk
•  400 m (437 yds) per 100 g
•  shown in colour 07 Miriam

*Note:* You may use any 100 g skein of fingering - sock - 4-ply yarn...see chart in PDF for amounts.

**Needles**
•  3 mm 40 cm (US 2.5 16”) circs
•  3 mm 40 cm (US 2.5) dpns
or size needed to obtain gauge

*Notes:* US 2.5 needles can be hard to find - you can use a US 3 instead. Some knitters prefer to use a smaller size needle for the ribbed brim only (like a 2.5 mm/US 2).

**Other Materials**
•  tapestry needle or teeny crochet hook (for weaving in ends), stitch marker


Looking for a matching cowl? Try the [Sockhead Cowl!][2]

Or maybe some matching gloves: [Pioneer Gloves!][3]

**Abbreviations**

BOR = beginning of round
circ(s) = circular needle(s)
CO = cast on
dpn(s) = double pointed needle(s)
dec(s) = decrease(s)
K = knit
K2tog = knit two stitches together (decrease)
m = marker
P = purl
patt = pattern
pm = place marker
rem = remaining
rep = repeat
rnd(s) = round(s)
sl m = slip marker
st(s) = stitche(s)
St st = stockinette stitch / stocking stitch
tog = together

  [1]: http://www.ravelry.com/patterns/library/pioneer-gloves
  [2]: http://www.ravelry.com/patterns/library/sockhead-cowl
  [3]: http://www.ravelry.com/patterns/library/pioneer-gloves

packs.yarn_name: Blue Moon Fiber Arts Socks that Rock Lightweight

pattern_author.name: Kelly McClure
pattern_author.notes:
![Bohoknits Logo FINAL WEB TEENY by half 1 inch.jpg][1]

[Browse Bohoknits stitch markers HERE!][2]

[Patterns now available on Payhip!][3]

Hello from Kelly McClure of Bohoknits! I design comprehensive knitting and crochet patterns from a country homestead in Prince Edward County, Ontario (Canada). I'm also passionate about dyeing yarn and making cute stitch markers.

Bohoknits originated in a camper van while traveling off-grid across Canada. Since I had no access to patterns (before iphones), I began to create my own. Now, my designs have appeared in stores all across North America and I have also taught knitting locally and in Nepal! I love how yarn and bits of fluff creates community and ties us together in invisible and also tangible ways. Fibre friends are the best friends!

When I’m not obsessing about yarn, stitches and all things fibre-related, I’m drinking tea, treasure hunting or walking in the woods with my dog, Lois, and little boy, Cameron.

I care about creating easy-to-follow patterns. All of my designs are test knit, but please contact me if you notice any mistakes.

**::::: BUY 3 patterns GET $3 OFF! :::::** No coupon code needed.

Email **kelly@bohoknits.com** or pm me on Ravelry with questions. You can also find @Bohoknits on Instagram.

If you are interested in **TRANSLATING** a pattern please contact me.

[Interviews and Collaboration Partners][4]

Happy knitting everyone! xo


  [2]: 


  [1]: /forum-images/bohoknits/52w1v-httamd
  [2]: https://www.etsy.com/ca/shop/Bohoknits
  [3]: https://payhip.com/Bohoknits
  [4]: http://bohoknits.blogspot.com/2021/02/blog-post.html

users.about_me:
My mother taught me to knit when I was a wee little thing and I taught myself to crochet in university. I have knit my way from the east coast to the west coast and if living in the chilliest parts of Canada has taught me anything, it's how to stay warm!

I spend most of my time knitting and designing. Other important obsessions of mine include tea drinking, sewing, traveling, reading, treasure hunting and running around with my puppy, Lois.
users.about_me_html:
My mother taught me to knit when I was a wee little thing and I taught myself to crochet in university. I have knit my way from the east coast to the west coast and if living in the chilliest parts of Canada has taught me anything, it’s how to stay warm!

I spend most of my time knitting and designing. Other important obsessions of mine include tea drinking, sewing, traveling, reading, treasure hunting and running around with my puppy, Lois.
